globalError.title: Something went wrong
globalError.lead: An unexpected error occurred. You can try going back or reloading the page.

nav.betabar: This service is in beta — please report issues on <forumLink>the Forum</forumLink>.

home.headline: Let's see if your Matrix server is reachable.
home.lead: Type your homeserver's domain below. We run a few checks from outside your network and walk you through anything that needs attention.

form.domainHint: The bit after the colon in your Matrix handle. If you're @alice:example.com, your domain is example.com.
form.statsLabel: Contribute this result to the public statistics page. We hash your domain before storing — never the raw name.

checks.title: What this checks
checks.step1Detail: Federation handshake on port 8448 or its delegated equivalent.
checks.step2Detail: Client-server API discovery and version probe.

help.title: Need a hand?
help.body: The docs walk through running a homeserver gently — no jargon required up front.

footer.meta: A free, open-source diagnostic tool for Matrix homeserver operators. Self-hostable under AGPL-3.0.

loggedOut.headline: Know the moment your server goes down.
loggedOut.lead: Add any number of Matrix homeservers to watch. We check federation every five minutes and email you when something breaks. You get a reminder every 12 hours while it stays down, and a confirmation when it recovers.

failure.title: Instant failure notification
failure.body: One email when federation breaks, then a reminder every 12 hours while it stays down.

recovery.title: Recovery confirmation
recovery.body: A follow-up when the server comes back so you know it's resolved.

recipients.title: Multiple recipients
recipients.body: Notify any set of verified addresses — ops team, on-call, or just yourself.

loggedOut.ctaNote: Your account is used only to deliver alert emails — no Matrix login required.

authed.headline: Server alerts
authed.lead: Get a quiet email the moment a homeserver becomes unreachable — and another when it recovers. Watch as many servers as you like; notify any combination of addresses for each. Email-only for now; Matrix-room delivery is on the roadmap.

form.title: Add a new alert
form.description: We probe the server every five minutes. The first failure triggers a notification; we won't spam you while it stays down.
form.notifyHint: Pick one or more. Add additional addresses from your account settings.

table.footerNote: One address can be on any number of alerts. Removing an alert never deletes the address itself.

edit.headline: Edit alert for
edit.lead: Pick which changes you want to hear about, who should hear about them, and how often. Saving overwrites the existing alert.
edit.watchDescription: You can mix and match. We send one email per event, never one per check.

uptime.title: Connectivity / downtime
uptime.detail: Notify when federation or the client-server API stops responding. Probed every 5 minutes.
uptime.hint: The original alert. Most people want this.

rename.title: Server name change
rename.detail: Notify if the homeserver implementation reported via /_matrix/federation/v1/version changes — e.g. a Synapse server suddenly identifies as Dendrite.
rename.hint: Useful for spotting account takeovers or accidental rollouts.

version.title: Version update
version.detail: Notify when the version string reported by /_matrix/federation/v1/version changes — typically an upgrade.
version.hint: Quiet by default — most useful for operators tracking peer servers.

tlsChange.title: TLS certificate change
tlsChange.detail: Notify if the TLS certificate fingerprint changes for any federation or client endpoint.
tlsChange.hint: Flags re-issued or rotated certificates. Some renewals are expected; this is a heads-up, not a problem.

tlsExpiry.title: TLS certificate expiry (≤ 14 days)
tlsExpiry.detail: Notify when any certificate in the chain has 14 days or fewer remaining. Repeats daily once tripped.
tlsExpiry.hint: A safety net even if your renewal cron usually handles it.

edit.notifyHint: Tap to toggle. Manage the address list itself from your account settings.

quietHours.description: Hold notifications during this window, applied to each recipient in their own timezone. Critical downtime always sends regardless. Each recipient's timezone comes from their account email settings.

webhooks.title: Webhook endpoints
webhooks.description: Receive event payloads as signed HTTP POST requests — useful for integrations with PagerDuty, Slack, or custom automation.
webhooks.createHint: You can add an HMAC secret and configure additional options from the edit page after saving.
webhooks.noDeliveries: No deliveries yet — use the Test button to send a ping.

register.eyebrow: Create an account · for alert notifications
register.headline: Set up alerts in two minutes.
register.lead: An account is only used to deliver downtime alerts for the homeservers you watch. It never talks to Matrix and never sees the contents of your rooms.
register.emailHint: We'll send a verification link here. Your primary contact for everything alert-related.
register.passwordHint: At least 8 characters with an uppercase letter, lowercase letter, number and one special character.

weak.label: Weak
weak.hint: Add a number and a special character.

fair.label: Fair
fair.hint: Mix in uppercase letters to strengthen it.

good.label: Good
good.hint: Looks reasonable. A few more characters helps.

strong.label: Strong
strong.hint: Great — this password is hard to guess.

register.privacyNote: By continuing you agree to the <privacyLink>privacy policy</privacyLink>. No marketing email, ever — only the alerts you configure.

login.headline: Welcome back.
login.lead: An account is only used for downtime alerts.
login.magicDescription: We'll email a one-time link that signs you in without a password. Valid for 1 hour.
login.createAccountMagic: Don't have an account? Create one
login.disclaimer: The Connectivity Tester does not log in with Matrix; this account exists only to deliver alert emails for the homeservers you watch.

sponsor.text: This service is free to use. If it's saved you a headache, consider supporting development.

account.title: Your account
account.leadAfter: . This account is used only to deliver homeserver downtime alerts — it is not a Matrix account and never talks to your homeserver.

emails.title: Email addresses
emails.description: Your primary address is used to sign in. Any verified address can be picked as a recipient when configuring an alert. Adding an address sends a one-click verification email; it stays in a pending state until you click the link.
emails.addDescription: We'll send a verification link valid for 24 hours.

password.title: Password
password.hasPasswordDescription: Change the password used for email-and-password sign-in. We use this alongside magic links — either is enough on its own to get in.
password.noPasswordDescription: You're currently using magic links only. Set up a password to enable email-and-password sign-in.
password.tooWeak: ✗ Too weak — aim for at least Fair
password.setupSuccess: Check your email — a setup link has been sent.

export.title: Download your data
export.description: A single JSON file containing everything we hold about you: account record, email addresses, alert subscriptions and notification history.
export.gdpr: Provided under GDPR Article 20 · Generated on demand · Typically < 50 KB.

delete.title: Delete account

items.profile: your profile and all email addresses;

delete.statsNote: Public, aggregated statistics you opted into are anonymous and remain in place.

results.title: Results for {{serverName}}
results.lead: Federation report for <mono>{{serverName}}</mono>.{{ipSuffix}} Check the verdict, any problems, and the full technical breakdown below.

pending.title: Running federation test…
pending.lead: Checking DNS, TLS, signing keys and server connectivity. This usually takes a few seconds.

error.title: Could not load results
error.lead: The federation test for <mono>{{serverName}}</mono> could not complete.
error.networkBody: The API server returned an error or could not be reached. It may be temporarily unavailable — please try again in a moment.

verdict.okBody: Federation checks passed — all server-to-server communication requirements are met.
verdict.warnBodyRetry: One or more endpoints required a retry to succeed. Remote servers may experience instability on their first connection attempt.
verdict.warnBodySplitBrain: Federation responded, but well-known was inconsistent across IPs (split-brain). Check the problems section below.
verdict.badBody: Federation checks failed. See the problems section below for details and suggested fixes.

problems.title: Problems & how to fix them
problems.noneBody: If a future test surfaces an issue, it'll show up here first — with a step-by-step explanation, not a stack trace.
problems.splitBrain: Split-brain: well-known responses inconsistent across IPs
problems.retryNeeded: One or more endpoints required a retry to succeed
problems.serverNameMismatch: {{ip}}: server name mismatch in signing keys
problems.ed25519NonCanonical: {{ip}}: non-canonical JSON numbers in key response
problems.serverVersionParse: {{ip}}: server version string could not be parsed
problems.connectionErrorHint: Check that your Matrix server is running, port 8448 (or your delegated port) is open, and not blocked by a firewall. <docsLink>Network troubleshooting guide →</docsLink>
problems.splitBrainHint: Your IPs are returning different m.server delegation values — remote servers will route inconsistently. Ensure all server IPs serve identical content at /.well-known/matrix/server. Well-known delegation guide →
problems.retryNeededHint: A transient failure occurred on the first connection attempt and resolved on retry. Remote servers connecting for the first time may hit this once — monitor for recurrence.
problems.serverNameMismatchHint: The signing key response does not include {{serverName}} as the server name. Verify your homeserver's server_name setting matches the domain being tested and that /_matrix/key/v2/server returns the correct name. Federation guide →
problems.invalidCertHint: Federation requires a valid TLS certificate from a publicly trusted CA (e.g. Let's Encrypt) covering your domain. Self-signed or expired certificates are rejected by other homeservers. <docsLink>TLS certificates guide →</docsLink>
problems.expiredKeysHint: Your signing keys have a valid_until_ts in the past. Other homeservers cache your keys and will refuse messages signed with expired keys. Restarting your homeserver typically rotates them automatically. Federation guide →
problems.noEd25519KeyHint: Every Matrix homeserver must publish at least one Ed25519 key at /_matrix/key/v2/server. Check that your homeserver's signing key is configured and the key file is readable. Federation guide →
problems.ed25519FailedHint: One or more Ed25519 signing keys failed verification. The server may be presenting stale, mismatched, or corrupted key material. Check your homeserver's signing key files and configuration. <docsLink>Federation guide →</docsLink>
problems.ed25519NonCanonicalHint: The server's /_matrix/key/v2/server response contains floating-point numbers, -0, or integers outside the Matrix-safe range [-(2^53)+1, (2^53)-1]. This violates the Matrix specification's canonical JSON rules. The server operator should update their homeserver software — this usually indicates an outdated or non-compliant implementation. Matrix spec →
problems.serverVersionParseHint: The value returned by /_matrix/federation/v1/version does not match the expected format. This is informational — custom builds may use non-standard version strings and federation still works normally.
problems.csApiUnavailable: Client-server API not reachable from this browser
problems.csApiUnavailableHint: This test couldn't reach your server's client API (/_matrix/client/versions) from the browser. This is often caused by missing CORS headers — Matrix clients connect differently and may still work fine. If you intend to support web clients, check your CORS configuration. CORS guide →
problems.wellKnownErrorHint: The federation well-known probe failed for this IP. Other servers trying to discover your federation endpoint via this IP will fall back to SRV or direct port 8448, which may succeed — but inconsistency across IPs can cause hard-to-diagnose federation issues. <docsLink>Well-known delegation guide →</docsLink>
problems.srvTargetError: SRV target {{target}} could not be resolved
problems.srvTargetErrorHint: One of your SRV records points to a hostname that could not be resolved to an IP address. Servers following SRV-based discovery may fail to connect on those targets. Verify that the SRV target resolves correctly or remove the broken record. <docsLink>Network troubleshooting guide →</docsLink>
problems.tlsOldVersionHint: The Matrix specification recommends TLS 1.2 or later. Older versions (TLS 1.0, TLS 1.1) have known weaknesses and are rejected by many modern homeservers. Update your server's TLS configuration to require TLS 1.2 at minimum. <docsLink>TLS certificates guide →</docsLink>
problems.csApiCorsBlocked: Client-server API is missing CORS headers
problems.csApiCorsBlockedHint: Your server's /_matrix/client/versions endpoint responded, but without an Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * header. Browsers are blocked from reading the response, so web Matrix clients cannot connect. Add the CORS header to your client-server API. CORS guide →
problems.rtcCorsBlocked: MatrixRTC transports endpoint is missing CORS headers
problems.rtcCorsBlockedHint: Your server's RTC transports endpoint (/_matrix/client/v1/rtc/transports) responded but without an Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * header. Web-based MatrixRTC clients cannot negotiate transport configuration. Add the CORS header to this endpoint. CORS guide →
problems.msc3266CorsBlocked: Room summary endpoint is missing CORS headers
problems.msc3266CorsBlockedHint: Your server's room-summary endpoint responded but without CORS headers. Web clients relying on MSC3266 room previews (used by Element Web and others) cannot access this endpoint. Add Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * to your client-server API configuration. CORS guide →
problems.supportCorsBlockedHint: Your server returned a response for /.well-known/matrix/support, but it does not include an Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * header. Browsers cannot read the response, so support contacts and the security disclosure page will not appear in web-based Matrix tools. Add the CORS header to this endpoint. Support endpoint guide →
problems.oidcCorsBlocked: OIDC auth issuer endpoint is missing CORS headers
problems.oidcCorsBlockedHint: Your server's /_matrix/client/v1/auth_issuer endpoint responded but without an Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * header. Web-based Matrix clients using OIDC authentication cannot discover the issuer. Add the CORS header to this endpoint. CORS guide →
problems.oidcDiscoveryUnreachableHint: Your server advertises OIDC at /_matrix/client/v1/auth_issuer, but the OpenID Connect discovery document at {{issuerUrl}}/.well-known/openid-configuration could not be fetched. Web clients performing OIDC login will fail. Ensure the OIDC provider (e.g. MAS) is running and its discovery endpoint is publicly reachable.
problems.oidcIssuerMismatchHint: The issuer field in the OpenID Connect discovery document does not match the URL it was fetched from. This violates the OIDC specification — clients may reject the discovery document. Ensure the OIDC provider is configured with the correct issuer URL.
problems.oidcJwksUnreachableHint: The JWKS endpoint (jwks_uri) from the OIDC discovery document could not be reached. Clients need this endpoint to validate tokens — OIDC logins will fail. Check that the JWKS URL is publicly accessible.

support.title: Moderation & security contacts
support.intro: Published via MSC1929 by the homeserver operator. If you're reporting abuse, harassment, or a security issue, these are the right addresses.
support.copyHint: Click an address to copy it, or tap the email to open your mail client.

errors.dnsNoRecord: DNS lookup failed — no A record found
errors.dnsNoRecordHint: The domain's A (IPv4) record is either missing or not yet propagated. Check your DNS provider's control panel and ensure an A record exists pointing to your server. DNS changes can take up to 48 hours to propagate. Verify with: dig DOMAIN A. Read the network troubleshooting guide →
errors.connRefused: Connection refused — server is not accepting connections
errors.connRefusedHint: Your server is reachable by DNS but actively refused the connection. Confirm that your Matrix server process is running and that port 8448 (or your delegated port) is open and reachable from the internet. Check your firewall rules. <docsLink>Read the network troubleshooting guide →</docsLink>
errors.timeout: Connection timed out — server did not respond
errors.timeoutHint: The server could be reached by DNS but did not respond in time. It may be overloaded, unreachable from the outside, or behind a firewall that silently drops packets rather than refusing them. <docsLink>Read the network troubleshooting guide →</docsLink>
errors.tlsHint: Check that your certificate is valid, not expired, and covers the correct hostnames. Make sure your server presents the full certificate chain. <docsLink>Read the federation troubleshooting guide →</docsLink>
errors.srvToCname: SRV record target is a CNAME — invalid DNS
errors.srvToCnameHint: Your SRV record target is a CNAME alias. The Matrix spec requires SRV targets to resolve directly to A or AAAA records, not via aliases. Update your DNS so the SRV target points to the server's actual hostname instead of a CNAME. <docsLink>Network troubleshooting guide →</docsLink>
errors.srvTimeoutHint: The DNS SRV lookup for your server timed out. The test falls back to A/AAAA resolution but SRV-based delegation may not be working correctly. Check your DNS provider's SRV record configuration and TTLs. <docsLink>Network troubleshooting guide →</docsLink>
errors.invalidServerNameHint: The server name does not appear to be a valid Matrix server domain. Verify you have entered a correct hostname and try again. <docsLink>Federation setup guide →</docsLink>

technical.title: Technical details
technical.intro: The sections below are mainly useful for server administrators and protocol developers. Click any one to expand.

csApi.title: Client-Server API overview
csApi.hint: Supported API versions and experimental features
csApi.unavailable: Client-server API unavailable or did not respond.

oidc.title: OIDC / Matrix 2.0 auth
oidc.hint: MSC3861 — delegated authentication via OpenID Connect
oidc.notConfiguredDetail: This server does not advertise an OIDC issuer via /.well-known/matrix/client or /_matrix/client/v1/auth_issuer. It uses the native Matrix auth flow (password, SSO, or token).
oidc.issuerMismatchWarning: Issuer field in discovery document does not match the issuer URL

rtc.title: MatrixRTC readiness
rtc.transportsEmpty: Endpoint responds but lists no transport types

statistics.title: Statistics
statistics.lead: A look at how this instance is being used. Only servers whose owners opted in show up here. Domain names are stored as one-way hashes.
statistics.errorBody: The metrics endpoint returned an error. It may be temporarily unavailable — please try again in a moment.
statistics.disabledBody: The statistics feature is not enabled on this instance.
statistics.privacyBody: Server software with five tests or fewer is grouped as "Other" so we can't accidentally identify small homeservers. Failed tests count toward totals but don't appear in the software distribution.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.lead: This policy describes what data the Matrix Connection Tester collects, why it collects it, and what rights you have over it.

s1.title: 1. Who is the data controller?
s1.p1: The Matrix Connection Tester is free, open-source software (AGPL-3.0) that anyone can self-host. The data controller is the operator of the specific instance you are using.
s1.controllerEmailOnly: the controller can be reached at {{email}}.
s1.controllerUnknown: contact the administrator for controller details.
s1.noInfo: If you are unsure who operates this instance, check the footer links or contact the site administrator.

s21.title: 2.1 Federation tests — no account required
s21.p1: When you enter a homeserver domain and run a test, the domain is sent to the API server to perform federation checks. We do not store the raw domain name.
s21.p2: If you tick "contribute to public statistics" on the home page (the checkbox is off by default), we record the result (pass / fail) and software version information. The domain name is always stored as a salted BLAKE3 hash — keyed with a server-side secret salt and irreversible. The raw domain is never written to storage.

s22.title: 2.2 Alert account
s22.p1: Creating an account to receive downtime alerts involves processing the following data:
s22.email: Email address(es) — your primary address is used for account verification, magic-link sign-in, password reset, and alert notifications. You may add additional addresses, each with its own notification preference.
s22.name: Display name (optional) — shown in the UI only.
s22.password: Password — stored as an Argon2id hash. Your plaintext password is never stored or transmitted after hashing.
s22.timezone: Timezone — an IANA timezone name (e.g. Europe/Berlin) used to interpret your quiet-hours settings.
s22.alerts: Alert configurations — the homeserver domains you choose to watch, together with notification preferences and quiet hours.
s22.history: Notification history — a rolling log of the last 50 events per alert (failures, recoveries, and similar).
s22.webhooks: Webhook endpoints (optional) — HTTPS URLs you configure to receive event notifications as signed HTTP POST requests. Each webhook may have an optional HMAC-SHA256 secret for payload verification. Webhook secrets are stored server-side and are never returned in API responses.
s22.footer: We use this data solely to deliver the notifications you configure. We never send marketing email.

s23.title: 2.3 Technical / server logs
s23.p1: The server may record IP addresses in access logs for security and operational purposes. Log retention period is configured by the operator.

s24.title: 2.4 Usage analytics
s24.p1: This instance uses Plausible Analytics — a privacy-first, open-source analytics tool. Plausible collects only aggregate, anonymous statistics: page views, referrer, country-level location, device type, and browser family. No cookies are set. No personal data is stored. No cross-site tracking occurs.
s24.p2: Analytics data is sent to a self-hosted Plausible instance at {{host}}. Data never leaves the operator's own infrastructure.

s3.title: 3. Legal basis (GDPR)
s3.noWarranty: This service is provided on a best-effort, no-warranty basis. No uptime guarantees are made or implied.
s3.accountData: Account and alert data: your consent, given when you create an account and configure alerts. You may withdraw consent at any time by deleting your account.
s3.statsOptIn: Statistics opt-in: your explicit consent (you tick the checkbox per test run; the default is off).
s3.serverLogs: Server access logs: legitimate interest in security and operational stability.

s4.title: 4. Third-party processors
s4.p1: We do not sell or share personal data with third parties. Email notifications are routed through an email delivery provider configured by the operator; only your email address and the notification content are transmitted for each delivery.
s4.webhooks: Webhook notifications are delivered directly to the HTTPS URLs you configure. These may point to third-party services (e.g. Slack, PagerDuty, or custom endpoints). Each delivery transmits the event type, a timestamp, and the server name over HTTPS. No personal account information is included in webhook payloads.
s4.p2: No third-party analytics, advertising networks, or tracking pixels are present on this site.
s4.p2Analytics: No advertising networks or tracking pixels are present on this site. This instance uses a self-hosted Plausible Analytics instance for aggregate usage statistics — see section 2.4 for details.

s5.title: 5. Cookies and browser storage
s5.p1: This site does not use cookies. During the sign-in and registration flow, OAuth2 PKCE parameters (a one-time code verifier and state token) are held in sessionStorage for the duration of the browser tab session and are cleared when the tab is closed.
s5.p2: Your selected UI language is saved in localStorage so the site remembers your preference across visits. No personal data is stored there.

s6.title: 6. Data retention
s6.accountData: Account data (email addresses, display name, timezone, alert configurations): retained until you delete your account.
s6.alertHistory: Alert event history: rolling window of the last 50 events per alert; deleted with your account.
s6.emailLog: Email notification log: a record of each notification sent (type, server, timestamp) is automatically pruned after {{emailLogDays}} days and deleted immediately when you delete your account.
s6.webhookLog: Webhook delivery log: delivery records (status, attempt count, HTTP status code, timestamps) are retained for 30 days then pruned automatically. Full payloads are not stored in the delivery log.
s6.anonStats: Anonymised statistics (salted BLAKE3 hash + pass/fail + software version): retained for {{statsDays}} days; they cannot be linked back to you or your account.
s6.serverLogs: Server access logs: retention period set by the operator.

s7.title: 7. Your rights
s7.p1: Under GDPR (and equivalent legislation) you have the right to:
s7.access: Access — download everything we hold about you from Account → Download data. The export is a machine-readable JSON file (GDPR Article 20).
s7.deletion: Deletion — permanently delete your account and all associated data from Account → Delete account. This is irreversible. Anonymised statistics (hashed domain, pass/fail) are not linked to your account and remain in place.
s7.correction: Correction — update or add email addresses from Account → Email addresses.
s7.portability: Portability — the JSON export (Account → Download data) covers all structured personal data we hold.
s7.withdrawConsent: Withdraw consent — statistics consent is given per test run via the checkbox on the home page; simply leave it unticked on future runs.
s7.objectOrRestrict: Object or restrict — contact the operator if you believe processing is unlawful or want to raise a concern.
s7.complaint: You also have the right to lodge a complaint with your national data protection authority.

s8.title: 8. Changes to this policy
s8.p1: We may update this policy when the service changes. The "last updated" date at the top of this page will reflect any revision. Continued use of the service after a change constitutes acceptance of the updated policy.

s9.title: 9. Contact
s9.p1: For privacy enquiries or to exercise your rights, contact the operator of this instance.
s9.controllerEmail: The controller for this instance can be reached at {{email}}.

docs.editOnGithub: Spotted a mistake? Edit this page on GitHub
